heparin.alert: Weight-based dosing. Verify units (units/hr, NOT mg). Typical IV range: 10-25 units/kg/hr. PTT monitoring required.

warfarin.alert: Narrow therapeutic index. Typical 2-10mg PO daily. INR monitoring required. Check for food/drug interactions.

insulin.alert: Verify units and concentration. Regular insulin IV: typical 0.1 units/kg/hr. Always check glucose before admin. Independent double-check required.

norepinephrine.alert: Typical range 0.01-0.5 mcg/kg/min. Must run through central line (extravasation = tissue necrosis). Titrate to MAP target per order.

epinephrine.alert: Cardiac arrest: 1mg IV push q3-5min. Infusion: 0.01-0.2 mcg/kg/min. Verify route (IV push vs infusion — very different dosing).

vasopressin.alert: Fixed dose in septic shock: 0.03-0.04 units/min (NOT titrated). Cardiac arrest: 40 units IV push x1.

propofol.alert: Sedation: 5-50 mcg/kg/min. Monitor for propofol infusion syndrome (triglycerides, metabolic acidosis) with prolonged use >48hr.

fentanyl.alert: IV: 25-100 mcg q1-2hr PRN. Infusion: 25-200 mcg/hr. Patch: 12-100 mcg/hr (NOT for opioid-naive). Respiratory depression risk.

morphine.alert: IV: 1-4mg q2-4hr PRN. Caution in renal impairment (active metabolite accumulates). Respiratory depression risk.

hydromorphone.alert: IV: 0.2-1mg q2-3hr PRN. 5-7x more potent than morphine. Common dosing error: confusing with morphine doses.

potassium_chloride.alert: IV: max 10 mEq/hr peripheral, 20 mEq/hr central (with cardiac monitoring). NEVER IV push. Verify concentration.

alteplase.alert: Stroke: 0.9mg/kg (max 90mg), 10% bolus + 60min infusion. Verify ALL exclusion criteria before administration. Irreversible once given.

amiodarone.alert: Cardiac arrest: 300mg IV push, then 150mg. Infusion: 1mg/min x6hr, then 0.5mg/min x18hr. Monitor QTc. Multiple drug interactions.

rocuronium.alert: RSI: 1.2mg/kg IV push. Patient is PARALYZED — verify adequate sedation (RASS, BIS) before and during. Sugammadex for reversal.

succinylcholine.alert: RSI: 1-1.5mg/kg IV push. Contraindicated: hyperkalemia, burns >24hr, crush injuries, denervation. Shortest duration paralytic.

digoxin.alert: Narrow therapeutic index. Typical 0.125-0.25mg PO daily. Toxic level >2 ng/mL. Check K+ (hypokalemia potentiates toxicity).
